jokes.text: Found groceries on the street - 'Someone didn't want it'
jokes.setup: Jemaine excitedly shows off groceries he got for free
jokes.punchline: Reveals he found discarded food on the street and frames it as a positive
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery establishing their poverty while Jemaine remains optimistic about literal garbage

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Found groceries on the street - 'Someone didn't want it'

jokes.text: Bret about to spit out street sandwich: 'I was gonna spit it out, but I think I'll just eat it. We're poor, man.'
jokes.setup: Bret realizes he's eating discarded street food
jokes.punchline: Decides to keep eating it purely because of their financial situation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing their desperation while maintaining the show's understated tone

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I was gonna spit it out, but I think I'll just eat it. We're poor, man.

jokes.text: 'Your toothbrush jar and a camera phone' - listing pathetic possessions they've pawned
jokes.setup: Song about being poor and pawning everything
jokes.punchline: Specific mention of absurdly small items like a toothbrush jar
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about poverty with specific, relatable details

dialogue.line: Your toothbrush jar and a camera phone

jokes.text: 'You want to sit down but you've sold your chair / So you... you just stand there'
jokes.setup: Describing the hardships of inner-city poverty
jokes.punchline: Anti-climactic conclusion that you just stand there
jokes.explanation: Excellent build-up to mundane punchline, perfectly captures their understated style

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: You want to sit down but you've sold your chair

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: So you... you just stand there

jokes.text: 'Hey, man, I just want some muesli' - desperate plea to convenience store manager
jokes.setup: Being abused by manager while counting coins at 7-11
jokes.punchline: Pathetic defense focusing on wanting healthy cereal
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor showing their earnest nature even in degrading situations

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Hey, man, I just want some muesli

jokes.text: 'Considering secondhand underpants' / 'What happened to those other underpants you had?'
jokes.setup: Describing how desperate poverty affects decision-making
jokes.punchline: Escalates to considering used underwear, then questioning what happened to previous pairs
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation of poverty desperation with appropriately uncomfortable details

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Considering secondhand underpants

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: What happened to those other underpants you had?

jokes.text: 'So you think maybe you'll be a prostitute / Just to pay for your lessons, you're learning the flute'
jokes.setup: Considering prostitution due to financial desperation
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's to pay for flute lessons, not basic survival
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - starts dark then reveals absurdly mundane motivation

dialogue.line: So you think maybe you'll be a prostitute
Just to pay for your lessons, you're learning the flute

jokes.text: 'Ladies wouldn't pay you very much for this / Looks like you'll never be a concert flautist'
jokes.setup: Considering male prostitution to fund flute career
jokes.punchline: Realistically assessing low earning potential and career prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - their earnest self-assessment even in absurd situations

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Ladies wouldn't pay you very much for this

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Looks like you'll never be a concert flautist

jokes.text: 'Ladies wouldn't pay you very much for this / Looks like you'll never be a concert flautist'
jokes.setup: Following the prostitution to pay for flute lessons premise
jokes.punchline: Crushing both the prostitution dream AND the flute dream simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double-crushing of dreams. The matter-of-fact delivery of this devastating assessment is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Ladies wouldn't pay you very much for this

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Looks like you'll never be a concert flautist

jokes.text: Murray's obsession with toothbrush fence - '50 brushes now. Pretty impressive, huh?'
jokes.setup: Murray is reading about local attractions
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's fascinated by a fence made of 50 toothbrushes
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment for Murray's fascination with mundane New Zealand attractions

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 50 brushes now. Pretty impressive, huh?

jokes.text: Money box cost more than they had - '$4? I thought we had $10?' 'This box cost $6.'
jokes.setup: They need money and Murray has it in a special box
jokes.punchline: The box to hold money cost more than the money it contains
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of their backwards priorities and Murray's questionable management

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: '$4? I thought we had $10?'

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: 'This box cost $6.'

jokes.text: 'The bag was useless, Bret. It had a great big hole in it. Must have lost about $20 out of that bag.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why he replaced the bag with an expensive box
jokes.punchline: Lost more money through the bag than they currently have total
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the money box joke showing Murray's flawed logic

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: The bag was useless, Bret. It had a great big hole in it. Must have lost about $20 out of that bag.

jokes.text: Murray calculating his 12% management fee from $4.27 and getting confused
jokes.setup: Murray wants his percentage of their tiny remaining funds
jokes.punchline: Gets confused by basic math on pocket change
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Murray's petty incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Murray calculating his 12% management fee from $4.27 and getting confused

jokes.text: 'It is my last $50. Till Thursday, I mean, you know. Getting paid again, but...'
jokes.setup: Murray generously offers his own money
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's not that generous since he gets paid in a few days
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Murray's self-serving generosity

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: It is my last $50. Till Thursday, I mean, you know. Getting paid again, but...

jokes.text: Mousepad disaster - 'I've got so many mousepads at home because of your bad idea, Bret'
jokes.setup: Discussion of band merchandise sales
jokes.punchline: Murray blames Bret for mousepad idea that left him with useless inventory
jokes.explanation: Setup for running mousepad gag, establishes Murray's resentment

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I've got so many mousepads at home because of your bad idea, Bret

jokes.text: 'I've lined Toby's kennel with them. He keeps eating them. I tried to waterproof the kitchen.'
jokes.setup: Murray explaining what he does with useless mousepads
jokes.punchline: Absurd attempts to repurpose them, including his dog eating them
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the mousepad problem with specific absurd details

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I've lined Toby's kennel with them. He keeps eating them. I tried to waterproof the kitchen.

jokes.text: 'I've just got a couple of businessmen here' - Murray pretending the band are businessmen
jokes.setup: Prime minister's office calls during band meeting
jokes.punchline: Murray tries to sound professional by calling struggling musicians 'businessmen'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Murray's delusions about their professional status

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I've just got a couple of businessmen here

jokes.text: 'Everybody knows you manage a band during office hours, Murray.'
jokes.setup: Murray trying to appear professional on phone
jokes.punchline: Coworker immediately exposes his unprofessional behavior
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of Murray's pretense with economy and timing

dialogue.line: Everybody knows you manage a band during office hours, Murray.

jokes.text: 'I wear that T-shirt all the time.'
jokes.setup: Greg is being supportive of the band
jokes.punchline: Implies he's one of only two T-shirt sales, suggesting he bought it out of pity
jokes.explanation: Economical way to show their lack of real fan base while maintaining Greg's supportive character.

dialogue.speaker: Greg
dialogue.line: I wear that T-shirt all the time.

jokes.text: 'Sounds like something a lamppost could do' - Bret's response to sign-holding job
jokes.setup: Boss explaining the complex task of holding a sign
jokes.punchline: Bret accurately assesses that it requires no human intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan observation about the meaninglessness of the job

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Sounds like something a lamppost could do

jokes.text: 'You're sign material. This guy overthinks things... You cannot.'
jokes.setup: Boss deciding between Bret and Jemaine for sign job
jokes.punchline: Considers basic critical thinking a disqualifying flaw
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for exploitative boss who values mindlessness

dialogue.speaker: Eddie
dialogue.line: You're sign material. This guy overthinks things... You cannot.

jokes.text: 'What if a wind comes and blows it away? You ever think of that, Enrico Fermi?'
jokes.setup: Boss getting frustrated with Jemaine asking logical questions
jokes.punchline: Compares him to famous physicist for asking about wind
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist reference comparing basic practical concern to nuclear physics genius

dialogue.speaker: Eddie
dialogue.line: What if a wind comes and blows it away? You ever think of that, Enrico Fermi?

jokes.text: Bret's confusion about 'booties' vs 'boobies' - thinking of baby boots
jokes.setup: Writing lyrics about shaking body parts
jokes.punchline: Bret's innocent interpretation leads to babies in woolen boots
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bret's innocent misunderstanding of suggestive lyrics

dialogue.line: Bret's confusion about 'booties' vs 'boobies' - thinking of baby boots

jokes.text: 'Yeah, shut up, Bret' - Jemaine's response to people skills advice
jokes.setup: Bret suggests Jemaine work on his people skills
jokes.punchline: Jemaine immediately demonstrates poor people skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction showing exactly what Bret means about people skills

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, shut up, Bret

jokes.text: Jemaine's elaborate free time schedule - sitting and light reading
jokes.setup: Jemaine explaining his leisure activities after practice
jokes.punchline: His ambitious plan is literally just sitting and reading
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing how simple their lives are

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Jemaine's elaborate free time schedule - sitting and light reading

jokes.text: 'You're not working, you're just holding a sign.' 'This is my work. Get out of here.'
jokes.setup: Murray dismissing Bret's job during emergency meeting
jokes.punchline: Bret defensively insisting sign-holding is real work
jokes.explanation: Solid character conflict about the dignity of menial labor

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You're not working, you're just holding a sign.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: This is my work. Get out of here.

jokes.text: Chicken-egg circular logic about jobs vs gigs
jokes.setup: Murray and Bret arguing about prioritizing job vs band
jokes.punchline: Gets stuck in infinite loop of circular reasoning
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of circular logic that perfectly captures their predicament

jokes.text: 'The chicken, obviously.' 'Well, where did the chicken come from?' 'Oh.'
jokes.setup: Murray confidently dismissing the chicken-egg paradox
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizes he hasn't solved anything
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Murray's overconfidence followed by realization

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: The chicken, obviously.
Well, where did the chicken come from?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: 'You're the egg. You're a bad egg... You've derailed this meeting with another obscure comment.'
jokes.setup: Murray getting confused by the metaphor
jokes.punchline: Blames Bret for bringing up the analogy Murray just engaged with
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Murray's tendency to blame others for his confusion

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You're the egg. You're a bad egg... You've derailed this meeting with another obscure comment.

jokes.text: Hot dog sauce expectations - 'You'd expect three sauces for that, wouldn't you?'
jokes.setup: Murray buying a $2 hot dog with two sauces
jokes.punchline: Has strong opinions about sauce-to-price ratios
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Murray's petty grievances

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You'd expect three sauces for that, wouldn't you?

jokes.text: 'People who hold signs go on to hold many things' - Eddie's motivational speech
jokes.setup: Boss giving pep talk about sign-holding career prospects
jokes.punchline: The ultimate career achievement is holding different objects
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist motivation that treats holding things as career advancement

dialogue.speaker: Eddie
dialogue.line: People who hold signs go on to hold many things

jokes.text: Coco's name confusion - 'Brit like... like Brittany?'
jokes.setup: Bret introducing himself to attractive coworker
jokes.punchline: She keeps mishearing his simple name
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward introduction humor, competent but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Coco
dialogue.line: Brit like... like Brittany?

jokes.text: 'There's Vikings there, right?' - Coco's geography knowledge
jokes.setup: Discussing New Zealand
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong historical/geographical reference
jokes.explanation: Basic 'Americans don't know geography' humor, functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Coco
dialogue.line: There's Vikings there, right?

jokes.text: 'She's so hot she's making me sexist. Bitch.'
jokes.setup: Bret attracted to Coco but worried about being inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves his point by calling her a derogatory term
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic escalation - he becomes what he's trying to avoid being

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: She's so hot she's making me sexist. Bitch.

jokes.text: Boom King song - entire performance
jokes.setup: Bret performing a song using only the word 'boom' with various meanings
jokes.punchline: Complete song structure maintained while making no linguistic sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sustained absurdist performance. The commitment to the bit and musical structure while using essentially one word is exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Boom King song - entire performance

jokes.text: Murray's New Zealand tourism - 'Imagine that, a whole fence made out of toothbrushes. Over 50 toothbrushes.'
jokes.setup: Murray promoting New Zealand tourism
jokes.punchline: Presents pathetically small attraction as amazing achievement
jokes.explanation: Good callback that establishes Murray's sincere pride in mundane attractions

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Imagine that, a whole fence made out of toothbrushes. Over 50 toothbrushes.

jokes.text: Australia vs New Zealand rivalry - 'Got Ayer's Rock, mate. Big huge rock.' 'We've got a big... smaller rocks.'
jokes.setup: Australian bragging about their famous landmark
jokes.punchline: Murray's defensive response admits their rocks are smaller
jokes.explanation: Excellent competitive exchange showing Murray's patriotic inadequacy

dialogue.speaker: Maxwell
dialogue.line: Got Ayer's Rock, mate. Big huge rock.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: We've got a big... smaller rocks.

jokes.text: 'Is this your band, Murray? A guy and a tape? Classic.'
jokes.setup: Australian waiting to hear the New Zealand band
jokes.punchline: Devastating observation about their pathetic performance setup
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with economical devastating putdown

dialogue.speaker: Maxwell
dialogue.line: Is this your band, Murray? A guy and a tape? Classic.

jokes.text: Song lyrics substituting 'boobies' for 'booties' and babies shaking booties
jokes.setup: Performance of the song they were working on earlier
jokes.punchline: Includes Bret's confusing baby bootie lyrics
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that pays off earlier setup about lyric confusion

dialogue.line: Song lyrics substituting 'boobies' for 'booties' and babies shaking booties

jokes.text: 'There's very little difference between you and the tape, I noticed.'
jokes.setup: Murray comparing Bret to his tape replacement
jokes.punchline: Insults Bret by saying he's indistinguishable from recorded music
jokes.explanation: Good character insult that's both absurd and cutting

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: There's very little difference between you and the tape, I noticed.

jokes.text: Murray's fake firing bluff being exposed by Jemaine
jokes.setup: Murray dramatically firing Bret
jokes.punchline: Jemaine accidentally reveals it was planned as a fake threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Murray's incompetent manipulation tactics

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You're fired.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Wait, Murray, that's not what we planned.

jokes.text: 'No, 'cause you ruined my bluff. The whole bluff thing.'
jokes.setup: Murray explaining why Jemaine doesn't get a ride
jokes.punchline: Murray's petty punishment for having his authority undermined
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Murray's petty authority and his frustration at being exposed.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: No, 'cause you ruined my bluff. The whole bluff thing.

jokes.text: 'All your fans are gonna be devastated.' 'There's just you, Mel.' 'Yeah.'
jokes.setup: Mel expressing concern about fans' reaction to Bret leaving
jokes.punchline: Bret points out she's literally their only fan
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan reality check about their complete lack of success

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: All your fans are gonna be devastated.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: There's just you, Mel.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: Mel's sexual description of Bret's sign skills
jokes.setup: Mel complimenting Bret's work performance
jokes.punchline: Turns mundane sign-holding into inappropriately sexual description
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Mel's inappropriate romantic fixation

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Mel's sexual description of Bret's sign skills

jokes.text: Jemaine lying about getting a real gig to impress Mel
jokes.setup: Mel asking about future performances
jokes.punchline: Jemaine fabricates success to maintain dignity
jokes.explanation: Standard character moment showing pride overriding honesty

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: So, when's your next gig?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh, we've got a big one coming up. Really big. Corporate event. They're flying us out, putting us up in a hotel.

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Really? That's great!

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's gonna be huge.

jokes.text: 'The car could just burst into flames right now and this would be the way to go'
jokes.setup: Mel expressing happiness about the evening
jokes.punchline: Wishes for death as the perfect end to the night
jokes.explanation: Good dark character moment showing Mel's romantic intensity

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: The car could just burst into flames right now and this would be the way to go

jokes.text: 'Don't stand next to any big magnets.' 'Why would I stand next to big magnets, Murray?'
jokes.setup: Murray giving advice about protecting the tape
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's reasonable confusion about the practical concern
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment about treating tape like precious band member

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Don't stand next to any big magnets.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Why would I stand next to big magnets, Murray?

jokes.text: Tape getting tangled in zipper and Murray's '80s fix attempt
jokes.setup: The irreplaceable tape gets caught
jokes.punchline: Murray's confident repair method destroys their only band member
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy climax that destroys their tape replacement solution

jokes.text: 'Does this mean I'm not in a band anymore?'
jokes.setup: The tape is destroyed, leaving Jemaine alone
jokes.punchline: Innocent question about his solo status
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment capturing the absurdity of being in a one-person band

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Does this mean I'm not in a band anymore?

jokes.text: Bret's transformation claims - 'I've grown up. I've got a job. I've got a career. I drink coffee now.'
jokes.setup: Bret explaining why he can't return to the band
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly mundane 'adult' achievements ending with coffee
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of phony maturity claims ending with coffee as peak sophistication

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I've grown up. I've got a job. I've got a career. I drink coffee now.

jokes.text: Bret immediately spitting out coffee - 'Oh God, yuck.'
jokes.setup: Bret claiming he drinks coffee now as sign of maturity
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates he can't actually drink coffee
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction showing his pretense is completely hollow

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Oh God, yuck.

jokes.text: 'You've given up the dream, Bret!' 'I've woken up.'
jokes.setup: Jemaine's dramatic accusation about abandoning music
jokes.punchline: Bret's philosophical response reframing reality as awakening
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that captures the philosophical divide between dream and reality

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You've given up the dream, Bret!

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I've woken up.

jokes.text: Coco preferring bands to signs after Bret assumed the opposite
jokes.setup: Bret explaining his choice to focus on his sign career
jokes.punchline: Reveals the girl he likes prefers the thing he abandoned
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist that motivates his return to music

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I thought you'd prefer the signs.

dialogue.speaker: Coco
dialogue.line: No, I prefer bands.

jokes.text: 'It's not about talent. You're not the talented one. But you do look very similar.'
jokes.setup: Boss explaining hiring decision between Bret and replacement
jokes.punchline: Admits the choice was based purely on visual similarity, not merit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing boss's shallow hiring practices and casual cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Eddie
dialogue.line: It's not about talent. You're not the talented one. But you do look very similar.
